28.reason: [Error: Returns 0 on EOF, but the main loop checks for EOF (-1). This causes an infinite loop because 0 != EOF, so getWord is called repeatedly without reading new data, leading to Time Limit Exceeded.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Returns 0 on EOF, but the main loop checks for EOF (-1). This causes an infinite loop because 0 != EOF, so getWord is called repeatedly without reading new data, leading to Time Limit Exceeded.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Returns 0 on EOF, but the caller checks for EOF (-1), so this will never be detected as end-of-file.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:   // The function returns 0 on EOF, but the while loop in main checks for EOF (-1). This causes an infinite loop when the file ends, leading to Time Limit Exceeded.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Returns 0 on EOF. While this distinguishes from the success return value 1, the caller checks against EOF (-1), leading to the infinite loop mentioned above. It should return EOF.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Should return EOF instead of 0 to signal end-of-file correctly to the caller. Returning 0 causes infinite loop because 0 != EOF]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Should return EOF (or -1) to signal end-of-file, not 0. Returning 0 causes infinite loop because main loop condition expects EOF to break.]

18.reason: [getWord returns 0 on EOF, not -1, so condition always true causing infinite loop]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  The function getWord returns 0 on EOF, not EOF (-1). This condition will never be true, causing an infinite loop.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [getWord returns 0 on EOF, not -1, so condition always true causing infinite loop]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  The function getWord returns 0 on EOF, not EOF. This causes an infinite loop when EOF is reached because 0 != EOF (which is typically -1).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The function getWord returns 0 on EOF, not EOF. This causes an infinite loop when the end of file is reached, leading to Time Limit Exceeded. It should be != 0.
